Quickly Clear Old Form Data or Stale Email History from Your Browser

Browsers love to suggest every email and username you (or anyone using your computer) ever typed. This post covers the Shift+Delete trick to remove individual autocomplete entries in Firefox and Chrome without nuking your whole browsing history.
Fixing 'Password authentication is temporarily disabled as part of a brownout'
I tried to push code today and got "remote: Password authentication is temporarily disabled as part of a brownout." from GitHub. Here's how to fix it.
Homebrew: homebrew-core / homebrew-cask Is a Shallow Clone — How to Fix It

Recently when installing Homebrew, I started seeing "homebrew-core is a shallow clone." and "homebrew-cask is a shallow clone." errors during install. This post records the fix — two git fetch --unshallow commands you need to run manually to make the install complete.
Window Went Off-Screen on Mac or Windows? How to Pull It Back

Sometimes a window drifts off your screen and you can't grab it with the mouse. This post covers two quick fixes — Alt + Space → Move + arrow keys on Windows to drag any off-screen window back, and the Window → Zoom menu (or force-quit) on Mac.
Mac: "zsh: command not found: xxx" — Two Common Causes and Fixes

Hit "zsh: command not found: hexo" or similar on Mac after installing a global npm package? Two flavors of this issue, both common — one if you use nvm (npm prefix conflicting with nvm), one if you don't (zsh PATH not picking up npm's global bin directory). This post covers both fixes step by step.
Docker `Error while fetching server API version` on Mac — Here's the Fix
Updated macOS and Docker stopped running entirely. Here's how to fix the "Error while fetching server API version" issue.
Browser Forces Localhost to HTTPS? How to Clear HSTS in Chrome / Edge / Firefox

Web devs spend a lot of time on localhost and 127.0.0.1, but sometimes the browser forces HTTPS or throws "This site can't provide a secure connection" and "localhost refused to connect" errors. This post explains why — HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) — and how to clear it in Chrome, Edge, and Firefox.
Windows "running scripts is disabled on this system" — How to Fix PowerShell Execution Policy

Hit "running scripts is disabled on this system, so cannot load..." in Windows PowerShell? Windows defaults the Execution Policy to Restricted (especially on Windows 10 PowerShell), which blocks script execution as a security measure. This post explains the Execution Policy levels (AllSigned, Bypass, Default, RemoteSigned, Restricted, Undefined, Unrestricted) and how to switch to RemoteSigned so npm, vue, gulp, webpack, etc. work normally.
VSCode 1.43 Syntax Highlight Issue (and How to Fix It)
VSCode updated to 1.43 and suddenly syntax highlighting looks off. Not sure if it's a deliberate change or a bug, but here's how to restore the original highlighting.
Fixing 'Node Sass could not find a binding' in a Vue Project
I hit a `Node Sass could not find a binding for your current environment` error in a Vue CLI 2 project recently, so here's a quick note on how to fix it.
